interactions.note: NSAIDs may reduce the effectiveness of ACE inhibitors and increase risk of kidney problems.
Both are anticoagulants; combined use increases bleeding risk significantly.
Alcohol may increase risk of lactic acidosis with metformin.
SSRIs with tramadol increase serotonin syndrome risk.
Antibiotics may reduce birth control efficacy.
Clarithromycin inhibits statin metabolism; increases myopathy risk.
High potassium levels can increase digoxin toxicity.
